title: AFI UWR Score KAT Vectors v0 (Testnet-Provisional DRAFT)
description: DRAFT / NON-IMPLEMENTATION schema for the computeUwrScore known-answer-test (KAT) vector file of the version-pinned UWR profile uwr-weighted-lifts-v0.1 (UP-11). Vectors are PURE DATA: expected values are decision-anchored literals; nothing in afi-config executes the engine. KAT execution against afi-core code is the separately-authorized PR-UWR-KAT-EXEC (vendored-golden-with-source-pin pattern). All values testnet-provisional, not production scoring law.
$comment: Authorized by afi-governance/decisions/uwr-profile-pin-v0.1.md UP-12 (PR-UWR-CONFIG only). No tolerance/epsilon field exists in this format: floating-point comparison policy deliberately belongs to PR-UWR-KAT-EXEC, and additionalProperties:false rejects any attempt to add one here.
x-afiPartOf: AFI version-pinned UWR profile registry (uwr-profile-pin-v0.1, PR-UWR-CONFIG)
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/uwr-profile-pin-v0.1.md — UP-3 engine pin, UP-4 axes pin, UP-5 weights pin, UP-11 KAT/golden gate
afi-governance/decisions/math-authority-v0.1.md §3 — KAT vectors (pure data) live in afi-config; KAT execution is a separately-authorized afi-core PR

x-afiOpenItems.comparison-policy: Floating-point comparison policy for executing these vectors is PR-UWR-KAT-EXEC scope (afi-core); this file defines none.

x-afiStatus.description: Artifact lifecycle marker carried on the document itself per UP-12.

description.description: OPTIONAL free-form commentary on the vector set.

formula.const: uwrScore = (structureWeight*clamp01(structure) + executionWeight*clamp01(execution) + riskWeight*clamp01(risk) + insightWeight*clamp01(insight)) / (structureWeight + executionWeight + riskWeight + insightWeight)

comparisonPolicyNote.const: Expected values are decision-anchored data. Floating-point comparison policy is PR-UWR-KAT-EXEC scope (afi-core); this file deliberately defines none.

axes.description: Raw pre-clamp axis inputs (UP-4 axis set, order significant in serialization). Deliberately unbounded numbers: the engine clamps each axis to [0, 1] BEFORE averaging, and clamp vectors exercise out-of-range inputs.
